what_it_does: This file is a design document for a reusable build-preparation primitive called the Code-Archaeology Dragnet. It specifies how to produce a coverage-complete, queryable map of a codebase before significant builds, covering rationale, denominator rules, engine reuse, parser-first extraction, addressing, projection-space registration, a structured field index, the operational cascade, milestones, and open gates.
observations: Elevates a one-off task into a reusable build-prep primitive rather than a provider-registry one-off.
Insists that enumeration walk the real filesystem tree, including git-ignored content directories, instead of relying only on git-ls-files.
Uses deterministic parsers (Python ast, regex for JS/TS/sh/sql, JSON keys) for structural facts and reserves LLM use for interpretive fields.
Proposes a project-scoped code:// addressing scheme with a #run= fragment for versioning.
Requires registering a dedicated projections/code_archaeology.py space before embedding can succeed.
Proposes a separate namespaced field-index store for imports/writes/declares rather than polluting the shared marks.jsonl store.
Defines milestone M1 as a coverage proof independent of M2 field-indexing and M3 cross-project comparison.
Status is PRE-POSITIONED and run is gated on Tim scope-confirm plus lead chat-4b contention clearance.
Reports a denominator of 1,651 files in the company repo, with 683 Python files.
Includes a discipline guard warning that the primitive itself must not be built from partial information.
standouts: Coverage-correctness rule: the denominator is the real filesystem tree, with explicit inclusion of git-ignored content dirs and loud, reasoned exclusions.
Parser-first mandate: exact structural facts come from parsers, not LLMs, to prevent hallucinated imports from corrupting downstream maps.

First-class registry/type discoverability: parser detects registry-row sentinels so roles, projections, mark types, node types, decisions, flows, panels, providers, models, MCP tools, and address schemes become queryable.
Milestone separation keeps the coverage proof (M1) independent of the reusable field-index layer (M2) and defers cross-project machinery (M3) until a second repo exists.
conventions: Follows project documentation conventions with board://item reference, status, lane, seam, and law fields.
Reuses the existing cog.run_items engine and projection registry pattern.
Breaks from git-ls-files-only enumeration by requiring a filesystem walk with explicit git-ignored content inclusion.
Breaks from the legacy code://<stem>/<symbol> form by adding a project segment and requiring an explicit disambiguation marker.
concerns: The legacy versus archaeology code:// disambiguation is explicitly flagged as unresolved and requires lead/recollection confirmation before build.
Multiple build prerequisites (address parser, projection registration, field-index store) must all land before the M1 coverage proof is achievable.
Relies on chat-4b @ :8000 with think=false, noted as a contention requiring lead coordination.
The byte-chunk plus LLM fallback for non-Python files may reintroduce hallucination risk for structural facts in the tail.
The proposed field-index uses a linear-scan pattern that may face scalability concerns as the record count grows.
notes: A gated design for a coverage-first codebase mapping primitive that insists on real-tree enumeration, parser-exact structure, registered embedding space, and milestone-gated build to prevent over-engineering before proof.
questions: What is the exact explicit disambiguation marker for code:// addresses?
Which git-ignored content directories besides reference/ and source/ are included or excluded?
How does the parser detect registry-row sentinels for each registry type in practice?
What is the precise schema and API surface for the field-index read interface?
What threshold defines a 'significant build' that triggers running the dragnet?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to specify a reusable build-prep primitive for coverage-complete codebase maps; the document does exactly that in design form, so purpose and actual content align.
apparent_themes: Prove on one repo before generalizing
Separation of coverage proof from reusable elaborations
intention_signals: The document is trying to institutionalize a pre-build reconnaissance step so agents stop building from partial information, by making a complete, queryable substrate map a prerequisite for significant changes.
novelty: The design combines real-filesystem denominator enforcement, parser-first structural extraction, first-class registry-row discovery, and milestone-gated self-discipline, distinguishing it from typical code-search or embedding-only approaches.
contribution: A structured, milestone-gated design for a coverage-complete codebase mapping primitive, with explicit rules for enumeration, addressing, projection registration, and field-indexing, that a fused system could keep as a build-prep specification.
summary_for_embedding: Design document for the Code-Archaeology Dragnet, a reusable build-preparation primitive that produces a coverage-complete, queryable map of a codebase via real-filesystem enumeration, parser-first structural extraction, a registered embedding space, and milestone-gated implementation.
intention_for_embedding: To specify a pre-build reconnaissance primitive that prevents agents from constructing from partial information by providing a complete, queryable substrate map with exact structural facts and discoverable registry entries.
